title: Examples
text: vignette includes several examples estimating performance characteristics experimental kit testing samples one populations experimental test one reference tests. examples vignette begin data frame containing counts possible testing combinations populations. basic example includes obtaining data frame counts necessary use estimateSnSp function, please see Getting Started Vignette. notation used consistent found CVB STATWI0002. simplest case illustrated estimating sensitivity specificity 2–state experimental test using test results experimental kit obtained 2–state fallible reference test samples tested single population. time necessary perform optimizations slightly minute. optimization takes significantly longer estimating parameters 3–state experimental test optimization 4 parameters 3–state experimental test, rather 2 parameters 2–state experimental test. complex example 3–state experimental test, three 2–state fallible reference tests samples tested 3 populations. time necessary perform optimizations slightly less 12 minutes using Windows 10 platform Intel i7 processor. Code vignette run using version 0.6.9 DiagTestKit package.

what: Example 1 — One, 2–state reference test, One population, 2–state experimental test
title: Examples
text: example, samples randomly selected single population tested one 2–state fallible reference test 2–state experimental test. , \(S_1\) = 2, \(S_2\) = 2 \(K\) = 4. 4 unique sets testing combinations. , probabilities associated suspect test result 0 experimental test method reference test method. Specifically, \(\phi_1\) = \(\psi_1\) = \(\phi_2\) = \(\psi_2\) = 0. example uses named data frames input variables Sn.ref Sp.ref.
code: ex1 <- estimateSnSp(dat = data1,           Sn.ref = data.frame(ref = c(0.90, 0)),           Sp.ref = data.frame(ref = c(0.99, 0)),           prev.pop = c(A = 0.80),           control = estimateSnSpControl(seed = 64725,                                         rep.iter = FALSE)) ## Warning in estimateSnSp(dat = data1, Sn.ref = ## data.frame(ref = c(0.9, 0)), : The data suggests ## a single population was tested ## The optimization has begun unique(ex1$detailOut$Converge) ## [1] 0 unique(ex1$detailOut$Message) ## [1] "CONVERGENCE: NORM OF PROJECTED GRADIENT <= PGTOL" ## [2] "CONVERGENCE: REL_REDUCTION_OF_F <= FACTR*EPSMCH" ex1 ## 1000  simulations  ## 95 % Interval Estimates ##  ##               Point.Estimate     Lower Upper ## Sn = P(T+|D+)      0.9449821 0.9019639     1 ## Sp = P(T-|D-)      0.9062769 0.7523346     1

previous_headings: Example 1 — One, 2–state reference test, One population, 2–state experimental test
title: Examples
text: confidence level can updated without requiring repeating simulations.
code: ex1_update <- updateAlpha(ex1, newAlpha = 0.01) ex1_update ## 1000  simulations  ## 99 % Interval Estimates ##  ##               Point.Estimate     Lower Upper ## Sn = P(T+|D+)      0.9449821 0.8848417     1 ## Sp = P(T-|D-)      0.9062769 0.7111645     1

what: Example 2 — One, 2–state reference test, Three populations, 2–state experimental test
title: Examples
text: example, samples randomly selected 3 populations tested one 2–state fallible reference test 2–state experimental test. , \(S_1\) = 2, \(S_2\) = 2 \(K\) = 4. 4 unique sets testing combinations, total 12 counts, one unique test combination three populations. probabilities associated suspect test result 0 experimental test method reference test method. Specifically, \(\psi_1\) = \(\phi_1\) = \(\psi_2\) = \(\phi_2\) = 0. example uses named vectors input variables Sn.ref Sp.ref reference test 2 states.
code: ex2 <- estimateSnSp(dat = data2,          Sn.ref = c(ref_result = 0.90),          Sp.ref = c(ref_result = 0.94),          prev.pop = c(A = 0.92, B = 0.20, C = 0.50),          control = estimateSnSpControl(seed = 4902342,                                        rep.iter = FALSE)) ## The optimization has begun unique(ex2$detailOut$Converge) ## [1] 0 unique(ex2$detailOut$Message) ## [1] "CONVERGENCE: NORM OF PROJECTED GRADIENT <= PGTOL" ex2 ## 1000  simulations  ## 95 % Interval Estimates ##  ##               Point.Estimate     Lower     Upper ## Sn = P(T+|D+)      0.8614248 0.8171083 0.9162939 ## Sp = P(T-|D-)      0.9818038 0.9260871 1.0000000

what: Example 3 — One, 3–state reference test, One population, 2–state experimental test
title: Examples
text: Samples randomly selected single population tested one 3–state fallible reference test 2–state experimental test. , \(S_1\) = 2, \(S_2\) = 3 \(K\) = 6. 6 unique sets testing combinations. probabilities associated suspect test result experimental test zero (.e. \(\psi_1\) = \(\phi_1\) = 0). reference test 3 states, input must include values \(\delta_2 = \frac{\psi_2}{1 - \pi_2}\) \(\gamma_2 = \frac{\phi_2}{1 - \theta_2}\).
code: ex3 <- estimateSnSp(dat = data3,         Sn.ref = data.frame(ref = c(0.99, 0.75)),         Sp.ref = data.frame(ref = c(0.85, 0.67)),         prev.pop = c(A = 0.92),         control = estimateSnSpControl(seed = 896421,                                       rep.iter = FALSE)) ## Warning in estimateSnSp(dat = data3, Sn.ref = ## data.frame(ref = c(0.99, 0.75)), : The data ## suggests a single population was tested ## The optimization has begun unique(ex3$detailOut$Converge) ## [1] 0 unique(ex3$detailOut$Message) ## [1] "CONVERGENCE: NORM OF PROJECTED GRADIENT <= PGTOL" ## [2] "CONVERGENCE: REL_REDUCTION_OF_F <= FACTR*EPSMCH" ex3 ## 1000  simulations  ## 95 % Interval Estimates ##  ##               Point.Estimate     Lower     Upper ## Sn = P(T+|D+)      0.9195444 0.8882768 0.9792288 ## Sp = P(T-|D-)      0.9211223 0.6734354 1.0000000

code: ex4 <- estimateSnSp(dat = data4,         Sn.ref = data.frame(ref = c(0.95, 0.55)),         Sp.ref = data.frame(ref = c(0.93, 0.48)),         prev.pop = c(A = 0.97, B = 0.25, C = 0.68),         control = estimateSnSpControl(seed = 6589732,                                     rep.iter = FALSE)) ## The optimization has begun unique(ex4$detailOut$Converge) ## [1] 0 unique(ex4$detailOut$Message) ## [1] "CONVERGENCE: NORM OF PROJECTED GRADIENT <= PGTOL" ex4 ## 1000  simulations  ## 95 % Interval Estimates ##  ##               Point.Estimate     Lower     Upper ## Sn = P(T+|D+)      0.9101593 0.8722713 0.9675238 ## Sp = P(T-|D-)      0.8802044 0.8221522 0.9373587

what: Example 5 — One, 2–state reference test, Two populations, 3–state experimental test
title: Examples
text: example, samples randomly selected two populations tested 3–state experimental test one 2–state reference test. 6 unique testing combinations (\(S_1\) = 3, \(S_2\) = 2 \(K\) = 6) total 12 counts samples obtained 2 populations. probabilities associated suspect test result experimental test (\(\psi_1\) \(\phi_1\)) included output (optimization occurs terms \(\delta_1\) \(\gamma_1\)). function provides message inform user experimental test 3 states optimization time consuming.
code: ex5 <- estimateSnSp(dat = data5,          Sn.ref = c(Ref1 = 0.90),          Sp.ref = c(Ref1 = 0.99),          prev.pop = c(A = 0.80, B = 0.90),          control = estimateSnSpControl(seed = 1249856,                                        rep.iter = FALSE)) ## Optimization is more time consuming for a 3-state experimental test, be patient! ## The optimization has begun unique(ex5$detailOut$Converge) ## [1] 0 unique(ex5$detailOut$Message) ## [1] "CONVERGENCE: NORM OF PROJECTED GRADIENT <= PGTOL" ## [2] "CONVERGENCE: REL_REDUCTION_OF_F <= FACTR*EPSMCH" ex5 ## 1000  simulations  ## 95 % Interval Estimates ##  ##               Point.Estimate     Lower      Upper ## Sn = P(T+|D+)     0.94126806 0.8937435 1.00000000 ## Sp = P(T-|D-)     0.87213528 0.7169586 1.00000000 ## P(T?|D+)          0.03792794 0.0000000 0.06171887 ## P(T?|D-)          0.04930292 0.0000000 0.16122575

what: Example 7 — Two, 3–state reference tests, Two populations, 2–state experimental
title: Examples
text: example, samples selected 2 populations tested two 3–state reference tests 2–state experimental test. , \(S_1\) = 2, \(S_2\) = 3, \(S_3\) = 3 \(K\) = 18. 18 unique sets testing combinations total 36 counts, one unique test combination 2 populations. probabilities associated suspect test result experimental test zero (.e. \(\psi_1\) = \(\phi_1\) = 0).
code: ex7 <- estimateSnSp(dat = data7,           Sn.ref = data.frame(ref1 = c(0.88, 0.75), ref2 = c(0.90, 0.55)),           Sp.ref = data.frame(ref1 = c(0.97, 0.6), ref2 = c(0.95, 0.5)),           prev.pop = c(A = 0.87, B = 0.35),           control = estimateSnSpControl(seed = 1937457,                                         rep.iter = FALSE)) ## The optimization has begun unique(ex7$detailOut$Converge) ## [1]  0 52 unique(ex7$detailOut$Message) ## [1] "CONVERGENCE: NORM OF PROJECTED GRADIENT <= PGTOL" ## [2] "CONVERGENCE: REL_REDUCTION_OF_F <= FACTR*EPSMCH"  ## [3] "ERROR: ABNORMAL_TERMINATION_IN_LNSRCH" ex7 ## 1000  simulations  ## 95 % Interval Estimates ##  ##               Point.Estimate     Lower Upper ## Sn = P(T+|D+)      0.9661330 0.9157368     1 ## Sp = P(T-|D-)      0.9215106 0.8715217     1

what: Example 8 — Three, 2–state reference tests, Three populations, 3–state experimental
title: Examples
text: example, samples randomly selected 3 populations tested three 2–state reference tests 3–state experimental test. , \(S_1\) = 3, \(S_2\) = 2, \(S_3\) = 2, \(S_4\) = 2 \(K\) = 24. 24 unique sets testing combinations total 72 counts, one unique test combination 3 populations. probabilities associated suspect test result experimental test (\(\psi_1\) \(\phi_1\)) included output. function provides message inform user experimental test 3 states optimization time consuming.
code: ex8 <- estimateSnSp(dat = data8,        Sn.ref = c(ref1_result = 0.92, ref2_result = 0.88, ref3_result = 0.85),        Sp.ref = c(ref1_result = 0.86, ref2_result = 0.90, ref3_result = 0.92),        prev.pop = c(A = 0.95, B = 0.62, C = 0.18),        control = estimateSnSpControl(seed = 865213,                                      rep.iter = FALSE)) ## Optimization is more time consuming for a 3-state experimental test, be patient! ## The optimization has begun unique(ex8$detailOut$Converge) ## [1] 0 unique(ex8$detailOut$Message) ## [1] "CONVERGENCE: REL_REDUCTION_OF_F <= FACTR*EPSMCH"  ## [2] "CONVERGENCE: NORM OF PROJECTED GRADIENT <= PGTOL" ex8 ## 1000  simulations  ## 95 % Interval Estimates ##  ##               Point.Estimate     Lower      Upper ## Sn = P(T+|D+)     0.96541704 0.8879949 1.00000000 ## Sp = P(T-|D-)     0.98351924 0.9016964 1.00000000 ## P(T?|D+)          0.02568427 0.0000000 0.06339616 ## P(T?|D-)          0.01534125 0.0000000 0.05604950

title: Getting Started
text: DiagTestKit package provides functions obtain point interval estimates diagnostic sensitivity specificity experimental diagnostic test kit one imperfect reference tests used. package also includes function estimate obtain confidence intervals diagnostic sensitivity specificity event infallible reference test used. technical details algorithm works provided STATWI0002. 2-state experimental test one fallible reference tests, default display show point interval estimates sensitivity (\(\pi_1\)) specificity (\(\theta_1\)). 3-state experimental test one fallible reference tests, default display show point interval estimates sensitivity (\(\pi_1\)), specificity (\(\theta_1\)), probability suspect disease positive (\(\psi_1\)) probability suspect disease negative (\(\phi_1\)). algorithm optimizes sum squared residuals respect \(\pi_1\), \(\theta_1\), \(\delta_1\), \(\gamma_1\) \(\delta_1\) \(\gamma_1\) express probability suspect result fraction non-correct test results. See STATWI0002 details. vignette provides step––step instructions using package obtain point interval estimates sensitivity (Sn = \(\pi\)) specificity (Sp = \(\theta\)) 2–state experimental test kit. DiagTestKit package requires functional installation R software. DiagTestKit package available via github. Code vignette run using version 0.6.9 DiagTestKit package.

title: Getting Started
text: data file titled dichotomoussensspec_deviceinfo.csv used illustrate use estimateSnSp function. data file includes 3 sample types, whole blood samples used demonstrate estimateSnSp function. Importing data illustrated completeness, dataset dat_dichot available within DiagTestKit package. raw data files follow formats Section 1.8 Section 1.9 Appendix 1 CVB Data Guide. table counts (dat_infal) available within “DiagTestKit” package used illustrate use cloppearSnSp function. cloppearSnSp provides binomial confidence interval using Clopper-Pearson method infallible reference test used.

title: Getting Started
code: library(ggplot2) library(DiagTestKit) data("ExampleData") dat <- dat_dichot infallible <- dat_infal

title: Getting Started
text: data input estimateSnSp function requires creating data frame includes count unique combination test results. data frame include zero counts possible test combinations observed, include zero counts impossible test combinations (structural zeros). Including non-structural zeros may achieved using .drop=FALSE setting within ddply. one way obtain data frame counts possible test combinations. Two possibilities illustrated . First, ddply function plyr package can used. require renaming columns manner recognized estimateSnSp. Namely, column name visual_read raw data file needs changed reflect response associated experimental kit. expression “exp” included column corresponding experimental test results. expression “ref” included columns corresponding reference test(s) results. Alternatively, data frame including counts possible testing combinations can obtained converting output table function base R data frame.
code: #Blood Samples blood <- ddply(.data = subset(dat, specimen == "wholeblood"),                   .variables = .(visual_read, ref_result),                   .drop = FALSE,                   .fun = summarize,                   Count = length(deviceID)) names(blood) <- c("Exp", "Ref1", "Count") blood2 <- data.frame(table(Exp = dat$visual_read[dat$specimen == "wholeblood"],                           Ref1 = dat$ref_result[dat$specimen == "wholeblood"]))

title: Getting Started
text: Use estimateSnSp function estimate sensitivity specificity 2–state experimental test. function 4 required inputs, data frame counts (dat), reasonable values based available information sensitivity reference test (Sn.ref), reasonable values based available information specificity reference test (Sp.ref), vector containing reasonable estimates prevalence population sampled. element prevalence vector named distinguish populations sample sets (.e. named vector). Two alternative methods inputting performance characteristics (sensitivity specificity) reference test available (illustrated ) 2–stage reference test, named vector named data frame. 3–stage reference test, probability suspect proportion incorrect test result (disease positive disease negative) must supplied. Therefore, one reference tests 3 stages, named data frame must used.

what: Use a named data frame for inputs of Sn.ref and Sp.ref
title: Getting Started
text: example illustrates use named data frame rather named vector input variables Sn.ref Sp.ref. output seen indicates column named “population” dataset treated sampled single population. function also provides updates every 50 iterations (simulation cycles). nsim specified input therefore default 1000 simulation cycles performed. , dataset blood used input data (dat). Sn.ref Sp.ref named data frames containing single column two rows. reference test 2 states, second row includes zero (suspect region). column named “Ref1” indicate inputs correspond first (, instance) reference test. prev.pop vector single named element. element name “” used arbitrary designation population (sample set) tested. ## optimization begun## following number iterations completed: 50## following number iterations completed: 100## following number iterations completed: 150\(\vdots\)## following number iterations completed: 1000
code: blood_SnSp <- estimateSnSp(dat = blood,                            Sn.ref = data.frame(Ref1 = c(0.95, 0)),                            Sp.ref = data.frame(Ref1 = c(0.98, 0)),                            prev.pop = c(A = 0.70)) ## Warning in estimateSnSp(dat = blood, Sn.ref = ## data.frame(Ref1 = c(0.95, : The data suggests a ## single population was tested ## The optimization has begun ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 50 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 100 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 150 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 200 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 250 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 300 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 350 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 400 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 450 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 500 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 550 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 600 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 650 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 700 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 750 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 800 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 850 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 900 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 950 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 1000 blood_SnSp ## 1000  simulations  ## 95 % Interval Estimates ##  ##               Point.Estimate     Lower Upper ## Sn = P(T+|D+)      1.0000000 0.9931197     1 ## Sp = P(T-|D-)      0.9553267 0.8446853     1

what: Use a named vector for inputs of Sn.ref and Sp.ref
title: Getting Started
text: example single 2–state reference test, named vector can used input variables Sn.ref Sp.ref (rather named data frame). one reference tests 3–state tests (includes suspect region), using named vector option. Using named vectors Sn.ref Sp.ref illustrated data frame obtained converting output table function (blood2). example shows additional code reduce frequency messages regarding number iterations completed.
code: blood2_SnSp <- estimateSnSp(dat = blood2,                            Sn.ref = c(Ref1 = 0.95),                            Sp.ref = c(Ref1 = 0.98),                            prev.pop = c(A = 0.70),                            control = estimateSnSpControl(iter.n = 250)) ## Warning in estimateSnSp(dat = blood2, Sn.ref = ## c(Ref1 = 0.95), Sp.ref = c(Ref1 = 0.98), : The ## data suggests a single population was tested ## The optimization has begun ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 250 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 500 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 750 ## The following is the number of iterations completed: 1000 blood2_SnSp ## 1000  simulations  ## 95 % Interval Estimates ##  ##               Point.Estimate     Lower Upper ## Sn = P(T+|D+)      1.0000000 0.9906616     1 ## Sp = P(T-|D-)      0.9526165 0.8447273     1

title: Getting Started
text: output 2 examples (shown ) exactly seed random number generation set examples, seed used can viewed using following code. output can forced controlling seed. illustrated using second dataset seed used first call estimateSnSp. message regarding number iterations suppressed example.
code: blood_SnSp$input$seed ## [1] 33274 blood2_SnSp$input$seed ## [1] 53652 blood2_a_SnSp <-   estimateSnSp(dat = blood2,       Sn.ref = c(Ref1 = 0.95),       Sp.ref = c(Ref1 = 0.98),       prev.pop = c(A = 0.70),       control = estimateSnSpControl(seed = blood_SnSp$input$seed,                                       rep.iter = FALSE)) ## Warning in estimateSnSp(dat = blood2, Sn.ref = ## c(Ref1 = 0.95), Sp.ref = c(Ref1 = 0.98), : The ## data suggests a single population was tested ## The optimization has begun blood2_a_SnSp ## 1000  simulations  ## 95 % Interval Estimates ##  ##               Point.Estimate     Lower Upper ## Sn = P(T+|D+)      1.0000000 0.9931197     1 ## Sp = P(T-|D-)      0.9553267 0.8446853     1

title: Getting Started
text: Prior reporting point interval estimates, verify optimization convergence instance. numeric value 0 indicates optimization converged. , see converged can check convergence message well.
code: unique(blood_SnSp$detailOut$Converge) ## [1] 0 unique(blood_SnSp$detailOut$Message) ## [1] "CONVERGENCE: NORM OF PROJECTED GRADIENT <= PGTOL"

title: Getting Started
text: optimized values sensitivity experimental kit can viewed using histogram.  optimized values specificity experimental kit can viewed using histogram.

title: Getting Started
text: Similarly, simulated values sensitivity reference test can extracted using blood_SnSp$input$Sn.sims. simulated distribution sensitivity reference test displayed histogram.  simulated values specificity reference test can extracted using blood_SnSp$input$Sp.sims. simulated distribution specificity reference displayed histogram.  #Infallible Reference Test Use cloppearSnSp function estimate sensitivity specificity 2–state experimental test infallible reference test used determine true disease status sample. function one required input, data frame counts (dat).

title: Getting Started
text: default function estimate sensitivity. total number positive samples tested can extracted using infal_Sn$data$Total.Positive number positive samples tested positive experimental test can obtained using infal_Sn$data$Test.Positive. sensitivity estimate can obtained infal_Sn$calcVal$Sn lower upper confidence limits can obtained using infal_Sn$calcVal$Sn.LL infal_Sn$calcVal$Sn.UL, respectively.
code: infal_Sn <- cloppearSnSp(dat = infallible) infal_Sn ## [1] "Sn = P(T+|D+): 0.987013 (95% CI: 0.953876, 0.998423)"

title: Getting Started
text: function estimate specificity est.Sn = FALSE. Similarly, total number negative samples tested can extracted using infal_Sp$data$Total.Negative number negative samples tested negative experimental test can obtained using infal_Sp$data$Test.Negative. specificity estimate can obtained infal_Sp$calcVal$Sp lower upper confidence limits can obtained using infal_Sp$calcVal$Sp.LL infal_Sp$calcVal$Sp.UL, respectively.
code: infal_Sp <- cloppearSnSp(dat = infallible,                          est.Sn = FALSE) infal_Sp ## [1] "Sp = P(T-|D-): 0.970297 (95% CI: 0.915643, 0.993832)"

what: Binomial confidence interval, Clopper-Pearson method. — cloppearSnSp
title: Binomial confidence interval, Clopper-Pearson method. — cloppearSnSp
text: Evaluate binomial confidence interval using Clopper-Pearson method. function written CVB Statistics estimate sensitivity specificity experimental diagnostic test kit accordance CVB STATWI0002.

title: Binomial confidence interval, Clopper-Pearson method. — cloppearSnSp
code: cloppearSnSp(dat, alpha = 0.05, est.Sn = TRUE)

title: Binomial confidence interval, Clopper-Pearson method. — cloppearSnSp
text: dat data.frame  data frame column experimental test results, column infallible reference test results,  column corresponding count.  column name experimental test results must contatin 'exp' column name infallible reference test results must include 'ref'.  counts last column. alpha Complement confidence level. est.Sn logical (TRUE/FALSE) Indicating sensitivity confidence interval supplied (TRUE) specificity confidence interval supplied (FALSE).

title: Binomial confidence interval, Clopper-Pearson method. — cloppearSnSp
text: object type cp extends list.   calcVal Named vector point estimates estimated simulated intervals. See . data Test Total values data. See . alpha Complement confidence interval provided .

title: Binomial confidence interval, Clopper-Pearson method. — cloppearSnSp
text: calcVal list following elements Sn Sensitivity estimate. Sn.LL Lower confidence limit sensitivity. Sn.UL Upper confidence limit sensitivity. data list following elements Test.Positive Number experimental test positives. Total.Positive Total number positive samples.

title: Binomial confidence interval, Clopper-Pearson method. — cloppearSnSp
text: calcVal list following elements Sp Specificity estimate. Sp.LL Lower confidence limit specificity. Sp.UL Upper confidence limit specificity. data list following elements Test.Negative Number experimental test negatives. Total.Negative Total number negative samples. matrix single row.  est.Sn = T columns correspond number experimental test positives, total number positive samples, sensitivity, lower confidence limit sensitivity upper confidence limit sensitivity. est.Sn=F, columns correspond number experimental test negatives, total number negative samples, specificity, lower confidence limit specificity upper confidence limit specificity.

title: Binomial confidence interval, Clopper-Pearson method. — cloppearSnSp
text: Clopper CJ, Pearson ES, 1934. use confidence fiducial limits illustrated case binomial. Biometrika 26:404-413.

title: Binomial confidence interval, Clopper-Pearson method. — cloppearSnSp
text: DiagTestKit-package

title: Obtain cell counts — .cell_counts
text: function creates expected cell counts (probabilities) specific test pattern based diagnostic characteristics reference test(s) experimental test.

title: Obtain cell counts — .cell_counts
code: .cell_counts(   SnR,   SpR,   Prev,   SnE,   SpE,   sus.perc,   N_mat,   nstates,   suspect2staterows,   X,   Xpos,   Xsus,   Xneg,   ncells,   ntests )

title: Obtain cell counts — .cell_counts
text: SnR data.frame  column corresponds one reference test. Row 1 contains sensitivity reference test(s). Row 2 contains probability suspect result fraction non-correct test result. value 0 1 (inclusive). Namely, P(T? | D+) = \(\psi\) = \(\delta\) * (1 - \(\pi\)) \(\delta\) second row given column (reference test).  \(\delta =   \frac{\psi}{(1 - \pi)}\).  Use zero 2-state test (.e. suspect region). SpR data.frame column corresponds one reference test. Row 1 contains specificity reference test. Row 2 contains probability suspect result fraction non-correct test result.  value 0 1 (inclusive). Namely, P(T? | D-) = \(\phi\) = \(\gamma\) * (1 - \(\theta\)) \(\gamma\) second row given column (reference test). \(\gamma =   \frac{\phi}{(1 - \theta)}\).  Use zero 2-state test (.e. suspect region). Prev vector  named vector containing prevalence population sampled. SnE Sensitivity experimental test kit. SpE Specificity experimental test kit. sus.perc vector vector containing 2 elements, c(\(\delta\), \(\gamma\)) experimental test kit.  vector zeros 2-state experimental kit. \(\delta\) \(\gamma\) values 0 1 (inclusive) corresponding proportion remaining probability (.e. 1 - \(\pi\) 1 - \(\theta\)) suspect (\(\psi\) \(\phi\)).  \(\delta = \frac{\psi}{(1-\pi)}\) \(\gamma = \frac{\phi}{(1-\theta)}\). N_mat matrix Needs filled nstates vector vector length one greater number reference tests.  first element number states experimental test remaining entries number states reference test (using ordering SnR SpR). suspect2staterows Needs filled . X Needs filled . Xpos Needs filled . Xsus Needs filled . Xneg Needs filled . ncells Needs filled . ntests Needs filled .

title: Obtain cell counts — .cell_counts
text: vector vector expected counts corresponding properties reference experimental tests.  expected counts obtained based conditional independence assumption test methods.

title: Create Triangular Distribution — .create_triangle_dist
text: Creates discrete step/triangular distribution can used sample values sensitivity specificity reference test prevalence population.

title: Create Triangular Distribution — .create_triangle_dist
code: .create_triangle_dist(   m,   w,   h,   threestate = FALSE,   suspect = 2/3,   step_size = 0.005,   p_proportion = TRUE )

title: Create Triangular Distribution — .create_triangle_dist
text: m point estimate parameter obtaining distribution, e.g. sensitivity, specificity, prevalence. w vector  vector provides half widths 3 regions, (w1 closest, w3 farthest). h vector  vector "y" (pseudo-value scaled probability) corresponding height shoulder height plateau. threestate logical (TRUE/FALSE) Indicates whether "suspect" region (.e. positive/suspect/negative). suspect fraction indicates percentage remaining probability assigned suspect region.  instance, function gives sensitivity probability "suspect" (1 - sensitivity) * suspect. step_size distance 'x' discrete distribution, resolution possible observations created distribution. p_proportion whether express 'p' proportion sum.

title: Create Triangular Distribution — .create_triangle_dist
text: data.frame 'x', 'y', 'p'.

title: Get Simulated Values — .get_simulated_values
text: Simulate values use optimization.  function used obtain draws distribution sensitivity specificity reference test prevalence population tested.

title: Get Simulated Values — .get_simulated_values
code: .get_simulated_values(means, distn, spread, nsim, step_size, prevalence)

title: Get Simulated Values — .get_simulated_values
text: means vector named vector containing point estimates prevalence population (prevalence = TRUE, see ) data frame column corresponds reference test rows sensitivity (\(\pi\)) \(\psi\) (specificity (\(\theta\))  \(\phi\)). distn vector  vector length means. Values may one NULL, 'beta', 'triangular'. NULL treated 'beta' spread vector vector length means. Values may one NULL, 'wide', 'medium', 'narrow'. NULL treated 'wide'. nsim number simulations draw sensitivity specificity distribution(s) reference test prevalence distribution population. step_size Provides level resolution values simulated triangular distribution. prevalence logical (TRUE/FALSE)  TRUE indicates function simulating values prevalence.  determine structure output.

title: Get Simulated Values — .get_simulated_values
text: final_mat matrix simulated values.  prevalence TRUE, final_mat number columns corresponding number populations sampled else prevalence FALSE, final_mat number columns twice number reference tests.  columns sensitivity (specificity) first reference test, probability suspect result fraction non-correct test result (.e. either \(\delta\) \(\gamma\)) first reference continues pattern reference tests.

title: Get Simulated Values — .get_simulated_values
text: DiagTestKit-package

title: Optimization of Sensitivity and Specificity — .get_values
text: Determine final optimized values sensitivity specificity experimental test kit (probability suspect given disease positive given disease negative 3-state kit).

title: Optimization of Sensitivity and Specificity — .get_values
code: .get_values(   dat,   SnR.vec,   SpR.vec,   prev.vec,   N.vec,   nstates,   tolerance,   rep.iter,   iter.n,   parm = NULL )

title: Optimization of Sensitivity and Specificity — .get_values
text: dat vector vector counts ordered manner consistent output .cell_counts function. SnR.vec data.frame  column corresponds one reference test.  Row 1 contains sensitivity reference test(s). Row 2 contains probability suspect result fraction non-correct test result. value 0 1 (inclusive). Namely, P(T? | D+) = \(\psi\) = \(\delta\) * (1 - \(\pi\)) \(\delta\) second row given column (reference test). \(\delta = \frac{\psi}{(1 - \pi)}\).  Use zero 2-state test (.e. suspect region). SpR.vec data.frame column corresponds one reference test.  Row 1 contains specificity reference test. Row 2 contains probability suspect result fraction non-correct test result.  value 0 1 (inclusive). Namely, P(T? | D-) = \(\phi\) = \(\gamma\) * (1 - \(\theta\)) \(\gamma\) second row given column (reference test). \(\gamma = \frac{\phi}{(1 - \theta)}\).  Use zero 2-state test (.e. suspect region). prev.vec vector  named vector containing prevalence population sampled. N.vec vector  named vector containing sample size population sampled. nstates vector vector length one number reference tests.  first element number states experimental test remaining entries number states reference test (using ordering SnR.vec SpR.vec). tolerance Setting limit pgtol used optim function 'L-BFGS-B' method. See also optim. rep.iter logical (TRUE/FALSE)  Indicates updates printed regarding number iterations completed. iter.n integer indicating frequency updates number iterations completed. parm vector  vector starting values used optimization passed .minimize_cell.  2-state experimental test, vector length 2 entries (\(\pi\), \(\theta\)). 3-state experimental test, vector length 4 entries (\(\pi\), \(\delta\), \(\theta\), \(\gamma\)). See also estimateSnSp.

title: Optimization of Sensitivity and Specificity — .get_values
text: list:  following returned 2 3-state experimental tests -- sens.final  vector optimized values sensitivity experimental test kit. spec.final  vector optimized values specificity experimental test kit. converge  vector entry integer code detailing convergence optimization iteration.  0 indicates successful completion. See also optim. message  vector  entry includes character string giving additional information returned optimizer NULL.  See also optim. three states -- \(\delta\)  vector optimized values probability suspect result fraction non-correct test result diseased samples. \(\gamma\) vector optimized value probability suspect result fraction non-correct test result non-diseased samples.

title: Optimization of Sensitivity and Specificity — .get_values
text: DiagTestKit-package

SpR data.frame column corresponds one reference test. Row 1 contains specificity reference test. Row 2 contains probability suspect result fraction non-correct test result.  value 0 1 (inclusive). title: Estimate Sensitivity and Specificity — estimateSnSp
text: function written CVB Statistics estimate sensitivity specificity experimental diagnostic test kit accordance CVB STATWI0002.

title: Estimate Sensitivity and Specificity — estimateSnSp
code: estimateSnSp(dat, Sn.ref, Sp.ref, prev.pop, nsim = 1000, control = NULL)

title: Estimate Sensitivity and Specificity — estimateSnSp
text: dat data.frame  data frame first column includes information population sampled (one population sampled).  next column possible outcomes experimental test followed one column possible outcomes reference test (one column per test).  last column data frame provides number samples pattern test outcomes.  columns must included order described.  one population sampled, column name column containing population information must 'population'.  column containing test results experimental test must 'exp' name, experimental, experiment, exp, Exp, etc.  column names containing reference test results much contain 'ref' name, Ref1, Ref2, ref1_results, Reference2, etc. Sn.ref data.frame  column corresponds one reference test.  Row 1 contains sensitivity reference test(s). Row 2 contains probability suspect result fraction non-correct test result. value 0 1 (inclusive). Namely, P(T? | D+) = \(\psi\) = \(\delta\) * (1 - \(\pi\)) \(\delta\) second row given column (reference test). \(\delta = \frac{\psi}{(1 - \pi)}\).  Use zero 2-state test (.e. suspect region).  Alternatively, reference tests 2-state tests, sensitivities can input named vector.  Specifically, element vector must given name includes 'ref' (see ) column names (names elements within vector) must match Sp.ref. Sp.ref data.frame column corresponds one reference test. Row 1 contains specificity reference test. Row 2 contains probability suspect result fraction non-correct test result.  value 0 1 (inclusive). Namely, P(T? | D-) = \(\phi\) = \(\gamma\) * (1 - \(\theta\)) \(\gamma\) second row given column (reference test). \(\gamma = \frac{\phi}{(1 - \theta)}\). Use zero 2-state test (.e. suspect region).  Alternatively, reference tests 2-state tests, specificity can can input named vector.  Specifically, element vector must given name includes 'ref' (see ) column names (names elements within vector) must match Sn.ref. prev.pop vector  named vector containing prevalence population sampled.  names vector must match population labels used 'dat'. nsim number simulations draw sensitivity specificity distribution(s) reference test prevalence distribution population. control list control values replace defaults. See estimateSnSpControl details.

title: Estimate Sensitivity and Specificity — estimateSnSp
text: object type snsp extends list.   calcVal Point estimates estimated simulated intervals properties experimental kit. See . detailOut Detailed output values. See . input Simulated values.  See .

title: Estimate Sensitivity and Specificity — estimateSnSp
text: list following values include following 2- 3-state experimental tests -- Nsim  Number simulations performed. Confidence   1 - \(\alpha\). SnPE  Sensitivity point estimate obtained median estimated values. SnInterval Estimated simulated interval sensitivity. SpPE Specificity point estimate obtained median estimated values. SpInterval Estimated simulated interval specificity. three states, list also include -- SusDisPosPE Point estimate probability test suspect given disease positive (\(\psi\)) median calculated values (\(\psi\) = \(\delta\)(1-\(\pi\))). SusDisPosInterval Estimated simulated interval probability test suspect given disease positive (\(\psi\)). SusDisNegPE Point estimate probability test suspect given disease negative (\(\phi\)) median calculated values (\(\phi\) = \(\gamma\)(1-\(\theta\))). SusDisNegInterval Estimated simulated interval probability test suspect given disease negative (\(\phi\)).

title: Estimate Sensitivity and Specificity — estimateSnSp
text: list following detailed output values include following 2- 3-state experimental tests -- Exp.Sn  vector optimized values sensitivity experimental test kit. Exp.Sp  vector optimized values specificity experimental test kit. Converge  vector entry integer code detailing convergence optimization iteration.  0 indicates successful completion. See also optim. Message  vector  entry includes character string providing additional information returned optimizer NULL.  See also optim. three states, list also include -- Exp.pos.p  vector optimized values proportion remaining probability (1-Sn) corresponds suspect region diseased samples, namely \(\delta\). Exp.sus.pos  vector values P(T? | D+) (\(\psi\)) calculated Exp.sn Exp.pos.p. P(T?|D+) = \(\delta\) * (1 - \(\pi\)). Exp.neg.p vector optimized value proportion remaining probability (1-Sp) corresponds suspect region non-diseased samples, namely \(\gamma\). Exp.sus.neg vector values P(T? | D-) (\(\phi\)) calculated Exp.sp Exp.neg.p. P(T?|D-) = \(\gamma\) * (1 - \(\theta\)).

title: Estimate Sensitivity and Specificity — estimateSnSp
text: list containing seed used simulated values. seed  seed used random generation distributions sensitivity specificity reference tests prevalence population.  See also set.seed Sn.sims  matrix simulated values sensitivity reference test \(\psi\) \(\psi\) specified second row Sn.ref (zero Sn.ref vector). first two columns correspond first reference test, columns 3 4 second reference test exists, etc. Sp.sims matrix simulated values specificity reference test \(\phi\) \(\phi\) specified second row Sp.ref (zero Sp.ref vector). first two columns correspond first reference test, columns 3 4 second reference test exists, etc. prev.sims  matrix simulated values prevalence population.  column correspond one population.

title: Estimate Sensitivity and Specificity — estimateSnSp
text: DiagTestKit-package

1000  simulations # 95 % Interval Estimates # #               Point.Estimate     Lower  Upper # Sn = P(T+|D+)      0.9449821 0.9019639      1 # Sp = P(T-|D-)      0.9062769 0.7523346      1   if (FALSE) { data.2 <- data.frame(Population = rep(LETTERS[1:3], each = 24),                      exp_result = rep(rep(                        c('negative', 'positive', 'suspect'), each = 8), 3),                      ref1_result = rep(rep(                        c('negative', 'positive'), each = 4), 9),                      ref2_result = rep(rep(                        c('negative', 'positive'), each = 2), 18),                      ref3_result = rep(c('negative', 'positive'), 36),                      count = c(3, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1, 5,                                1, 8, 11, 62, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0,                                2, 27, 2, 3, 0, 4, 0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1,                                4, 1, 6, 7, 41, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0,                                0, 2, 57, 5, 6, 1, 9, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0,                                0, 1, 0, 2, 2, 12, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0)) example.2 <- estimateSnSp(dat = data.2,   Sn.ref = data.frame(ref1 = c(0.92, 0),                       ref2 = c(0.88, 0),                       ref3 = c(0.85, 0)),   Sp.ref = data.frame(ref1 = c(0.86, 0),                       ref2 = c(0.90, 0),                       ref3 = c(0.92, 0)),   prev.pop = c(A = 0.95, B = 0.62, C = 0.18),   control = estimateSnSpControl(seed = 865213)) # 1000  simulations # 95 % Interval Estimates  #                Point.Estimate     Lower      Upper # Sn = P(T+|D+)      0.96541704 0.8879949 1.00000000 # Sp = P(T-|D-)      0.98351924 0.9016964 1.00000000 # SsP = P(T?|D+)     0.02568427 0.0000000 0.06339616 # SsN = P(T?|D-)     0.01534125 0.0000000 0.05604950 }

title: control values for estimateSnSp — estimateSnSpControl
text: values supplied function--call replace defaults list possible arguments returned. returned list used control argument function estimateSnSp.

title: control values for estimateSnSp — estimateSnSpControl
code: estimateSnSpControl(   seed = NULL,   Sn.distn = NULL,   Sn.spread = NULL,   Sp.distn = NULL,   Sp.spread = NULL,   prev.distn = NULL,   prev.spread = NULL,   tolerance = 0.001,   alpha = 0.05,   step.size = 1e-06,   parm = NULL,   rep.iter = TRUE,   iter.n = 50 )

title: control values for estimateSnSp — estimateSnSpControl
text: seed seed used random generation distributions sensitivity specificity reference tests prevalence population.  See also set.seed. Sn.distn vector  named vector length equal number reference tests.  Determines disibution used sampling sensitivity reference test.  Inputs 'beta' 'triangular'. Defaults 'beta' reference test. Sn.spread vector  named vector length equal number reference tests. Describes width distribution sensitivity reference test.  Inputs 'wide', 'medium', 'narrow'.  Defaults 'wide' reference test. Sp.distn vector  named vector length equal number reference tests.  Determines disibution used sampling specificity reference test.  Inputs 'beta' 'triangular'. Defaults 'beta' reference test. Sp.spread vector  named vector length equal number reference tests. Describes width distribution specificity reference test.  Inputs 'wide', 'medium', 'narrow'.  Defaults 'wide' reference test. prev.distn vector  named vector length equal number populations. Determines disibution used sampling prevalence population.  Inputs 'beta' 'triangular'. Defaults 'beta'. prev.spread vector  named vector length equal number populations. Describes width distribution prevalence population.  Inputs 'wide', 'medium', 'narrow'. Defaults 'wide' population. tolerance Setting limit pgtol used optim function 'L-BFGS-B' method. See also optim. Defaults 1E-03. alpha Significance levels.  Defaults 0.05. step.size Provides level resolution values simulated triangular distribution.  Defaults 1E-06. parm vector  Starting values optimization parameters experimental test.  experimental test 2 states, vector length two elements corresponding sensitivity specificity, respectively.  experimental test 3 states, vector length 4 elements corresponding sensitivity (\(\pi\)), proportion 1-Sn corresponding suspect region disease positive samples (\(\delta\)), specificity (\(\theta\)), proportion 1-Sp corresponding suspect region disease negative samples (\(\gamma\)).  values 0 1, inclusive. rep.iter logical (TRUE/FALSE) Indicates updates printed regarding number iterations completed.  Defaluts TRUE. iter.n integer indicating frequency updates number iterations completed.  Defaluts 50.

title: control values for estimateSnSp — estimateSnSpControl
text: list following elements (defined ): seed,   Sn.disn, Sn.spread, Sp.distn,  Sp.spread, prev.distn, prev.spread,   tolerance, step.size, parm
